title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for versioning, delete markers, object lock, and recovery and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for version and immutable-retention recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
List versions and delete markers, resolve current reads, restore by removing a marker or copying a version, and preserve audit identity.
Apply governance, compliance, legal hold, retention date, and authorized bypass by version with irreversible-risk review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Versioning State Transition · changed case · Quiz Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 3108 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Restore Drill Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 1871 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Restore Drill Evidence through the concrete version and immutable-retention recovery runbook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence, keep Restore Drill Evidence connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

At S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 1871 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run sampled restore drills with authorization, known bytes, metadata, dependencies, time objectives, audit evidence, and safe cleanup.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Restore Drill Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A documented restore command proves recovery without executing a drill.
